    31→class CircError(Exception):
    32→    def __init__(self, error_type: str, message: str, file: str = None, line: int = None, col: int = None):

    34→        self.error_type = error_type

    99→OPERATOR_ARITY = {
   100→    'NOT': (1, 1), 'BUF': (1, 1),
   101→    'AND': (2, None), 'OR': (2, None), 'XOR': (2, None),
   102→    'NAND': (2, None), 'NOR': (2, None), 'XNOR': (2, None)

   106→class Parser:
   107→    def __init__(self, content: str, filename: str):
   108→        self.filename = filename
   109→        self.lines = content.split('\n')
   110→
   111→    def parse(self) -> Circuit:
   112→        circuit = Circuit()
   113→        in_declarations = True
   114→
   115→        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(self.lines, 1):
   116→            stripped = raw_line.strip()
   117→            if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   118→                continue
   119→
   120→            if '[' in stripped or ']' in stripped:
   121→                bracket_pos = stripped.find('[') if '[' in stripped else stripped.find(']')
   122→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Arrays (brackets) are not supported in scalar .circ format",
   123→                               self.filename, line_num, bracket_pos + 1)
   124→
   125→            if self._is_declaration(stripped):
   126→                if not in_declarations:
   127→                    raise CircError("DeclarationAfterAssignmentError", "Declaration after assignment is not allowed",
   128→                                   self.filename, line_num, 1)
   129→                self._parse_declaration(stripped, circuit, line_num)
   130→            elif '=' in stripped:
   131→                in_declarations = False
   132→                self._parse_assignment(stripped, circuit, line_num, raw_line)
   133→            else:
   134→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {stripped}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   135→
   136→        return circuit
   137→
   138→    def _is_declaration(self, line: str) -> bool:
   139→        parts = line.split()
   140→        return '=' not in line and bool(parts) and parts[0].upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE')
   141→
   142→    def _parse_declaration(self, line: str, circuit: Circuit, line_num: int):
   143→        parts = line.split()
   144→        if not parts:
   145→            return
   146→
   147→        keyword = parts[0].upper()
   148→        names = parts[1:]
   149→        target_map = {'INPUT': circuit.inputs, 'OUTPUT': circuit.outputs, 'WIRE': circuit.wires}
   150→        if keyword not in target_map:
   151→            return
   152→        target = target_map[keyword]
   153→
   154→        existing_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   155→        for name in names:
   156→            if not self._is_valid_identifier(name):
   157→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier: {name}", self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   158→            if name in existing_names:
   159→                raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}", self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   160→            target.append(Signal(name))
   161→            existing_names.add(name)
   162→
   163→    def _is_valid_identifier(self, name: str) -> bool:
   164→        return bool(name) and not name[0].isdigit() and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in name)
   165→
   166→    def _parse_assignment(self, line: str, circuit: Circuit, line_num: int, raw_line: str):
   167→        eq_pos = line.find('=')
   168→        lhs = line[:eq_pos].strip()
   169→        rhs = line[eq_pos + 1:].strip()
   170→
   171→        if not lhs:
   172→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Missing left-hand side in assignment", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   173→        if not self._is_valid_identifier(lhs):
   174→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier on left-hand side: {lhs}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   175→
   176→        rhs_expr, _ = self._parse_expression(rhs, line_num, eq_pos + 2)
   177→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, raw_line.find(lhs) + 1))
   178→
   179→    def _parse_expression(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> tuple:
   180→        text = text.strip()
   181→        if not text:
   182→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   183→        if text == 'X':
   184→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Literal 'X' values are not allowed in .circ files", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   185→        if text in ('0', '1'):
   186→            return Literal(text), len(text)
   187→        if self._is_valid_identifier(text) and '(' not in text:
   188���            return Identifier(text), len(text)
   189→
   190→        match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\(', text)
   191→        if match:
   192→            op_name = match.group(1).upper()
   193→            op_start = match.end()
   194→
   195→            paren_count = 1
   196→            i = op_start
   197→            while i < len(text) and paren_count > 0:
   198→                if text[i] == '(':
   199→                    paren_count += 1
   200→                elif text[i] == ')':
   201→                    paren_count -= 1
   202→                i += 1
   203→
   204→            if paren_count != 0:
   205→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis in expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   206→
   207→            args_text = text[op_start:i-1]
   208→            args = self._parse_arguments(args_text, line_num, start_col + op_start)
   209→            return Call(op_name, args), i
   210→
   211→        raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid expression: {text}", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   212→
   213→    def _parse_arguments(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> list:
   214→        text = text.strip()
   215→        if not text:
   216→            return []
   217→
   218→        args = []
   219→        current = ""
   220→        paren_depth = 0
   221→        col = start_col
   222→
   223→        for i, char in enumerate(text):
   224→            if char == '(':
   225→                paren_depth += 1
   226→                current += char
   227→            elif char == ')':
   228→                paren_depth -= 1
   229→                current += char
   230→            elif char == ',' and paren_depth == 0:
   231→                if current.strip():
   232→                    expr, _ = self._parse_expression(current.strip(), line_num, col)
   233→                    args.append(expr)
   234→                current = ""
   235→                col = start_col + i + 1
   236→            else:
   237→                current += char
   238→
   239→        if current.strip():
   240→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(current.strip(), line_num, col)
   241→            args.append(expr)
   242→
   243→        return args
   244→
   245→
   246→class Validator:
   247→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
   248→        self.circuit = circuit
   249→        self.filename = filename
   250→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
   251→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
   252→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
   253→
   254→    def validate(self):
   255→        self._check_assignments()
   256→        self._check_cycles()
   257→
   258→    def _check_assignments(self):
   259→        assigned = set()
   260→        all_names = self.circuit.get_all_names()
   261→
   262→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   263→            if asn.lhs not in all_names:
   264→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   265→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
   266→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   267→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
   268→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   269→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
   270→            self._check_expression(asn.rhs, asn.line)
   271→
   272→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
   273→            if sig.name not in assigned:
   274→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
   275→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
   276→
   277→    def _check_expression(self, expr, line_num: int):
   278→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   279→            if expr.name not in self.circuit.get_all_names():
   280→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.filename, line_num)
   281→        elif isinstance(expr, Call):
   282→            self._check_operator_arity(expr, line_num)
   283→            for arg in expr.args:
   284→                self._check_expression(arg, line_num)
   285→
   286→    def _check_operator_arity(self, call: Call, line_num: int):
   287→        op = call.operator
   288→        num_args = len(call.args)
   289→
   290→        if op not in OPERATOR_ARITY:
   291→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Unknown operator: {op}", self.filename, line_num)
   292→
   293→        min_args, max_args = OPERATOR_ARITY[op]
   294→        if num_args < min_args or (max_args and num_args > max_args):
   295→            req = f"exactly {min_args}" if max_args == min_args else f"at least {min_args}"
   296→            raise CircError("ArityError", f"Operator {op} requires {req} argument{'s' if min_args > 1 else ''}, got {num_args}", self.filename, line_num)
   297→
   298→    def _check_cycles(self):
   299→        dependencies = {sig.name: set() for sig in self.circuit.inputs}
   300→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   301→            dependencies[asn.lhs] = self._get_dependencies(asn.rhs)
   302→
   303→        WHITE, GRAY, BLACK = 0, 1, 2
   304→        color = {name: WHITE for name in self.circuit.get_all_names()}
   305→
   306→        def dfs(node: str, path: list):
   307→            color[node] = GRAY
   308→            path.append(node)
   309→            for dep in dependencies.get(node, set()):
   310→                if color[dep] == GRAY:
   311→                    cycle_start = path.index(dep)
   312→                    return path[cycle_start:] + [dep]
   313→                if color[dep] == WHITE:
   314→                    result = dfs(dep, path)
   315→                    if result:
   316→                        return result
   317→            path.pop()
   318→            color[node] = BLACK
   319→            return None
   320→
   321→        for node in self.circuit.get_all_names():
   322→            if color[node] == WHITE:
   323→                cycle = dfs(node, [])
   324→                if cycle:
   325→                    raise CircError("CycleError", f"Cycle detected: {' -> '.join(cycle)}", self.filename)
   326→
   327→    def _get_dependencies(self, expr) -> set:
   328→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   329→            return {expr.name}
   330→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   331→            deps = set()
   332→            for arg in expr.args:
   333→                deps.update(self._get_dependencies(arg))
   334→            return deps
   335→        return set()
   336→
   337→
   338→class Evaluator:
   339→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
   340→        self.circuit = circuit
   341→        self.values = {}
   342→
   343→    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
   344→        self.values = dict(inputs)
   345→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   346→            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
   347→        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}
   348→
   349→    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> int:
   350→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   351→            return int(expr.value)
   352→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   353→            return self.values[expr.name]
   354→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   355→            args = [self._eval_expr(arg) for arg in expr.args]
   356→            return self._apply_operator(expr.operator, args)
   357→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   358→
   359→    def _apply_operator(self, op: str, args: list) -> int:
   360→        if op == 'NOT':
   361→            return 1 - args[0]
   362→        if op == 'BUF':
   363→            return args[0]
   364→        ops = {'AND': (operator.and_, 1), 'OR': (operator.or_, 0), 'XOR': (operator.xor, 0)}
   365→        if op in ops:
   366→            fn, init = ops[op]
   367→            result = init
   368→            for a in args:
   369→                result = fn(result, a)
   370→            return result
   371→        if op in ('NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
   372→            base_op = op[1:]
   373→            return 1 - self._apply_operator(base_op, args)
   374→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   375→
   376→
   377→def output_json(data: dict):
   378→    print(json.dumps(data, separators=(',', ':')))
   379→
   380→
   381→def load_circuit(filename: str, command: str, json_mode: bool):
   382→    """Read, parse, and validate a circuit file. Returns Circuit or exit code int."""
   383→    try:
   384→        with open(filename, 'r') as f:
   385→            content = f.read()
   386→    except (FileNotFoundError, IOError):
   387→        return output_error(CircError("FileNotFoundError", f"Cannot read file: {filename}", filename), command, json_mode)
   388→
   389→    try:
   390→        circuit = Parser(content, filename).parse()
   391→        Validator(circuit, filename).validate()
   392→    except CircError as e:
   393→        return output_error(e, command, json_mode)
   394→
   395→    return circuit
   396→
   397→
   398→def signal_list(signals):
   399→    return [{"name": s.name, "msb": s.msb, "lsb": s.lsb} for s in sorted(signals, key=lambda s: s.name)]
   400→
   401→
   402→def get_exit_code(error_type: str) -> int:
   403→    if error_type in VALIDATION_ERRORS:
   404→        return EXIT_VALIDATION_ERROR
   405→    return ERROR_EXIT_CODES.get(error_type, 1)
   406→
   407→
   408→def output_error(error, command: str, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   409→    exit_code = get_exit_code(error.error_type)
   410→    if json_mode:
   411→        output_json({"ok": False, "command": command, "exit_code": exit_code, "error": error.to_dict()})
   412→    else:
   413→        loc = ""
   414→        if hasattr(error, 'file') and error.file:
   415→            loc = f"{error.file}:"
   416→            if error.line:
   417→                loc += f"{error.line}:"
   418→                if error.col:
   419→                    loc += f"{error.col}:"
   420→            loc += " "
   421→        print(f"{loc}Error: {error.message}", file=sys.stderr)
   422→    return exit_code
   423→
   424→
   425→def check_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   426→    if not args:
   427→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   428→
   429→    filename = args[0]
   430→    result = load_circuit(filename, "check", json_mode)
   431→    if isinstance(result, int):
   432→        return result
   433→    circuit = result
   434→
   435→    inputs = signal_list(circuit.inputs)
   436→    outputs = signal_list(circuit.outputs)
   437→
   438→    if json_mode:
   439→        output_json({"ok": True, "command": "check", "format": "circ", "inputs": inputs, "outputs": outputs})
   440→    else:
   441→        print("Circuit is valid.")
   442→        print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in inputs)}")
   443→        print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in outputs)}")
   444→
   445→    return 0
   446→
   447→
   448→def eval_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   449→    if not args:
   450→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   451→
   452→    filename = args[0]
   453→
   454→    set_values = {}
   455→    default_value = None
   456→    allow_extra = False
   457→
   458→    i = 1
   459→    while i < len(args):
   460→        arg = args[i]
   461→        if arg == '--set':
   462→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
   463→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--set requires a name=value argument"), "eval", json_mode)
   464→            set_arg = args[i + 1]
   465→            if '=' not in set_arg:
   466→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --set format: {set_arg}, expected name=value"), "eval", json_mode)
   467→            name, value = set_arg.split('=', 1)
   468→            if value not in ('0', '1'):
   469→                return output_error(EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{name}': '{value}', must be 0 or 1"), "eval", json_mode)
   470→            set_values[name] = int(value)
   471→            i += 2
   472→        elif arg == '--default':
   473→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
   474→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--default requires a value (0 or 1)"), "eval", json_mode)
   475→            default_arg = args[i + 1]
   476→            if default_arg not in ('0', '1'):
   477→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --default value: {default_arg}, must be 0 or 1"), "eval", json_mode)
   478→            default_value = int(default_arg)
   479→            i += 2
   480→        elif arg == '--allow-extra':
   481→            allow_extra = True
   482→            i += 1
   483→        else:
   484→            return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Unknown option: {arg}"), "eval", json_mode)
   485→
   486→    result = load_circuit(filename, "eval", json_mode)
   487→    if isinstance(result, int):
   488→        return result
   489→    circuit = result
   490→
   491→    input_names = {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}
   492→
   493→    for name in set_values:
   494→        if name not in input_names and not allow_extra:
   495→            return output_error(EvalError("UnknownInputError", f"Unknown input: {name}"), "eval", json_mode)
   496→
   497→    missing_inputs = input_names - set(set_values.keys())
   498→    if missing_inputs and default_value is None:
   499→        return output_error(EvalError("MissingInputError", f"Missing input values for: {', '.join(sorted(missing_inputs))}"), "eval", json_mode)
   500→
   501→    inputs = {sig.name: set_values.get(sig.name, default_value) for sig in circuit.inputs if sig.name in set_values or default_value is not None}
   502→
   503→    try:
   504→        results = Evaluator(circuit).evaluate(inputs)
   505→    except Exception as e:
   506→        return output_error(CircError("InternalError", str(e)), "eval", json_mode)
   507→
   508→    sorted_outputs = sorted(results.items(), key=lambda x: x[0])
   509→
   510→    if json_mode:
   511→        output_list = [{"name": name, "msb": 0, "lsb": 0, "value": str(value)} for name, value in sorted_outputs]
   512→        output_json({"ok": True, "command": "eval", "mode": "2val", "radix": "bin", "outputs": output_list})
   513→    else:
   514→        for name, value in sorted_outputs:
   515→            print(f"{name}={value}")
   516→
   517→    return 0
   518→
   519→
   520→def main():
   521→    args = sys.argv[1:]
   522→
   523→    if '--help' in args:
   524→        print("""Usage: circopt.py [OPTIONS] <COMMAND> [ARGS]
   525→
   526→Options:
   527→  --help     Show this help message and exit
   528→  --version  Show version and exit
   529→  --json     Output in JSON format
   530→
   531→Commands:
   532→  check      Validate a .circ circuit file
   533→  eval       Evaluate a circuit with given inputs
   534→
   535→Run 'circopt.py <COMMAND> --help' for more information on a command.""")
   536→        return 0
   537→
   538→    if '--version' in args:
   539→        json_mode = '--json' in args
   540→        if json_mode:
   541→            output_json({"ok": True, "command": "__version__", "version": VERSION})
   542→        else:
   543→            print(VERSION)
   544→        return 0
   545→
   546→    json_mode = '--json' in args
   547→    args = [a for a in args if a != '--json']
   548→
   549→    if not args:
   550→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "No command provided. Use --help for usage information."), "__cli__", json_mode)
   551→
   552→    command, *command_args = args
   553→
   554→    if command == 'check':
   555→        return check_command(command_args, json_mode)
   556→    if command == 'eval':
   557→        return eval_command(command_args, json_mode)
   558→    return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Unknown command: {command}"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   559→
   560→
   561→if __name__ == '__main__':
   562→    sys.exit(main())
   563→

content.text:
## Comprehensive Summary of the Codebase

### File Structure

The codebase is very minimal with only two files at the root level:

```
/workspace/
  circopt.py          (main implementation - 562 lines)
  requirements.txt    (states no external dependencies)
  __pycache__/        (compiled Python bytecode)
```

---

### Main File: `/workspace/circopt.py`

This is a **Circuit Optimizer CLI tool** that parses, validates, and evaluates digital logic circuits defined in a custom `.circ` format.

#### **Version and Exit Codes** (lines 11-28)
- `VERSION = "1.0.0"`
- Exit codes defined:
  - `EXIT_PARSE_ERROR = 2`
  - `EXIT_VALIDATION_ERROR = 3`
  - `EXIT_INPUT_VALUE_ERROR = 2`
- `VALIDATION_ERRORS` set containing: `DeclarationAfterAssignmentError`, `DuplicateNameError`, `UndefinedNameError`, `UnassignedSignalError`, `InputAssignmentError`, `MultipleAssignmentError`, `ArityError`, `CycleError`
- `ERROR_EXIT_CODES` mapping error types to exit codes

---

### Error Types

#### **1. CircError Class** (lines 31-41)
Base exception for circuit-related errors with attributes:
- `error_type: str` - type identifier
- `message: str` - error description
- `file: str` - optional filename
- `line: int` - optional line number
- `col: int` - optional column number
- Has `to_dict()` method for JSON serialization

#### **2. EvalError Class** (lines 44-51)
Exception for evaluation-time errors:
- `error_type: str`
- `message: str`
- Has `to_dict()` method

---

### Data Structures (Dataclasses)

#### **Signal** (lines 54-58)
```python
@dataclass
class Signal:
    name: str
    msb: int = 0  # Most significant bit (for future multi-bit support)
    lsb: int = 0  # Least significant bit
```

#### **Identifier** (lines 83-85)
```python
@dataclass
class Identifier:
    name: str
```

#### **Literal** (lines 88-90)
```python
@dataclass
class Literal:
    value: str  # "0" or "1"
```

#### **Call** (lines 93-96)
```python
@dataclass
class Call:
    operator: str
    args: list  # List of Expr
```

#### **Assignment** (lines 64-69)
```python
@dataclass
class Assignment:
    lhs: str
    rhs: Expr
    line: int
    col: int
```

#### **Circuit** (lines 72-80)
```python
@dataclass
class Circuit:
    inputs: list   # List of Signal
    outputs: list  # List of Signal
    wires: list    # List of Signal
    assignments: list  # List of Assignment
```

---

### Parser Implementation (lines 106-243)

The `Parser` class handles parsing `.circ` files:

**Key Methods:**
- `__init__(content, filename)` - initializes with file content and name
- `parse()` - main parsing entry point, returns a `Circuit`
- `_is_declaration(line)` - checks if line is INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE declaration
- `_parse_declaration(line, circuit, line_num)` - parses signal declarations
- `_is_valid_identifier(name)` - validates identifier names
- `_parse_assignment(line, circuit, line_num, raw_line)` - parses assignment statements
- `_parse_expression(text, line_num, start_col)` - parses expressions (returns tuple of expr and length)
- `_parse_arguments(text, line_num, start_col)` - parses comma-separated arguments

**Parsing Rules:**
1. Declarations must come before assignments
2. Comments start with `#`
3. Arrays (brackets) are not supported
4. Literal 'X' values are not allowed
5. Identifiers cannot start with digits, can contain alphanumerics and underscores

---

### Validator Implementation (lines 246-335)

The `Validator` class validates the parsed circuit:

**Key Methods:**
- `validate()` - main validation entry point
- `_check_assignments()` - validates assignment rules
- `_check_expression(expr, line_num)` - validates expression references
- `_check_operator_arity(call, line_num)` - validates operator argument counts
- `_check_cycles()` - detects cycles using DFS (white-gray-black coloring)
- `_get_dependencies(expr)` - extracts dependencies from expressions

**Validation Checks:**
1. No assignments to undefined signals
2. No assignments to inputs
3. No multiple assignments to same signal
4. All outputs and wires must be assigned
5. All referenced signals must exist
6. Operators must have correct arity
7. No cycles in the circuit graph

**Operator Arity** (lines 99-103):
```python
OPERATOR_ARITY = {
    'NOT': (1, 1), 'BUF': (1, 1),
    'AND': (2, None), 'OR': (2, None), 'XOR': (2, None),
    'NAND': (2, None), 'NOR': (2, None), 'XNOR': (2, None)
}
```
- `(min_args, max_args)` where `None` means unlimited

---

### Evaluator Implementation (lines 338-374)

The `Evaluator` class evaluates circuits with given inputs:

**Key Methods:**
- `evaluate(inputs: dict) -> dict` - evaluates circuit, returns output values
- `_eval_expr(expr) -> int` - recursively evaluates expressions
- `_apply_operator(op, args) -> int` - applies logic gate operations

**Logic Operations:**
- `NOT`: 1 - arg
- `BUF`: arg (pass-through)
- `AND`: Reductive AND (init=1)
- `OR`: Reductive OR (init=0)
- `XOR`: Reductive XOR (init=0)
- `NAND`, `NOR`, `XNOR`: Negated versions of base operations

---

### CLI Commands (lines 425-558)

#### **check command** (lines 425-445)
- Validates a `.circ` file
- Reports inputs and outputs
- Usage: `circopt.py check <file.circ>`

#### **eval command** (lines 448-517)
- Evaluates circuit with given inputs
- Options:
  - `--set name=value` - set input value (0 or 1)
  - `--default 0|1` - default value for unspecified inputs
  - `--allow-extra` - allow extra input values
- Usage: `circopt.py eval <file.circ> [--set name=value]... [--default 0|1] [--allow-extra]`

#### **main()** (lines 520-562)
- Parses CLI arguments
- Supports `--help`, `--version`, `--json` global options
- Routes to appropriate command handler

---

### JSON Output Format

When `--json` flag is used, output follows this structure:
```json
{
  "ok": true/false,
  "command": "check|eval|...",
  "exit_code": 0,
  "error": {...},  // if ok is false
  "inputs": [...], // for check command
  "outputs": [...] // for both commands
}
```

---

### Requirements

The `/workspace/requirements.txt` file contains:
```
# No external dependencies required
```

The implementation uses only Python standard library modules:
- `sys`, `json`, `re`, `dataclasses`, `typing`, `operator`

---

### Key Design Patterns

1. **Separation of Concerns**: Parser, Validator, and Evaluator are separate classes
2. **Dataclasses**: Used for all AST nodes and data structures
3. **Error Handling**: Custom exception classes with location information
4. **Type Hints**: Used throughout the codebase
5. **Functional Operators**: Uses Python's `operator` module for gate operations
6. **Graph Algorithms**: DFS with coloring for cycle detection

   104→

   106→class Parser:
   107→    def __init__(self, content: str, filename: str):
   108→        self.filename = filename
   109→        self.lines = content.split('\n')
   110→
   111→    def parse(self) -> Circuit:
   112→        circuit = Circuit()
   113→        in_declarations = True
   114→
   115→        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(self.lines, 1):
   116→            stripped = raw_line.strip()
   117→            if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   118→                continue
   119→
   120→            if '[' in stripped or ']' in stripped:
   121→                bracket_pos = stripped.find('[') if '[' in stripped else stripped.find(']')
   122→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Arrays (brackets) are not supported in scalar .circ format",
   123→                               self.filename, line_num, bracket_pos + 1)
   124→
   125→            if self._is_declaration(stripped):

   127→                    raise CircError("DeclarationAfterAssignmentError", "Declaration after assignment is not allowed",
   128→                                   self.filename, line_num, 1)

   130→            elif '=' in stripped:
   131→                in_declarations = False
   132→                self._parse_assignment(stripped, circuit, line_num, raw_line)
   133→            else:
   134→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {stripped}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   135→
   136→        return circuit
   137→
   138→    def _is_declaration(self, line: str) -> bool:
   139→        parts = line.split()
   140→        return '=' not in line and bool(parts) and parts[0].upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE')
   141→
   142→    def _parse_declaration(self, line: str, circuit: Circuit, line_num: int):
   143→        parts = line.split()
   144→        if not parts:
   145→            return
   146→
   147→        keyword = parts[0].upper()
   148→        names = parts[1:]

   150→        if keyword not in target_map:
   151→            return
   152→        target = target_map[keyword]
   153→
   154→        existing_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   155→        for name in names:
   156→            if not self._is_valid_identifier(name):
   157→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier: {name}", self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   158→            if name in existing_names:
   159→                raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}", self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   160→            target.append(Signal(name))
   161→            existing_names.add(name)
   162→
   163→    def _is_valid_identifier(self, name: str) -> bool:
   164→        return bool(name) and not name[0].isdigit() and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in name)
   165→
   166→    def _parse_assignment(self, line: str, circuit: Circuit, line_num: int, raw_line: str):
   167→        eq_pos = line.find('=')

   169→        rhs = line[eq_pos + 1:].strip()

   171→        if not lhs:

   173→        if not self._is_valid_identifier(lhs):

   183→        if text == 'X':

   188���            return Identifier(text), len(text)
   189→
   190→        match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\(', text)
   191→        if match:
   192→            op_name = match.group(1).upper()
   193→            op_start = match.end()
   194→
   195→            paren_count = 1
   196→            i = op_start
   197→            while i < len(text) and paren_count > 0:
   198→                if text[i] == '(':
   199→                    paren_count += 1
   200→                elif text[i] == ')':
   201→                    paren_count -= 1
   202→                i += 1
   203→
   204→            if paren_count != 0:
   205→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis in expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   206→
   207→            args_text = text[op_start:i-1]
   208→            args = self._parse_arguments(args_text, line_num, start_col + op_start)
   209→            return Call(op_name, args), i
   210→
   211→        raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid expression: {text}", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   212→
   213→    def _parse_arguments(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> list:
   214→        text = text.strip()
   215→        if not text:
   216→            return []
   217→
   218→        args = []
   219→        current = ""
   220→        paren_depth = 0
   221→        col = start_col
   222→
   223→        for i, char in enumerate(text):
   224→            if char == '(':
   225→                paren_depth += 1
   226→                current += char
   227→            elif char == ')':
   228→                paren_depth -= 1
   229→                current += char
   230→            elif char == ',' and paren_depth == 0:
   231→                if current.strip():
   232→                    expr, _ = self._parse_expression(current.strip(), line_num, col)
   233→                    args.append(expr)
   234→                current = ""
   235→                col = start_col + i + 1
   236→            else:
   237→                current += char
   238→
   239→        if current.strip():
   240→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(current.strip(), line_num, col)
   241→            args.append(expr)
   242→
   243→        return args
   244→
   245→
   246→class Validator:
   247→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
   248→        self.circuit = circuit
   249→        self.filename = filename
   250→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
   251→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
   252→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
   253→
   254→    def validate(self):
   255→        self._check_assignments()
   256→        self._check_cycles()
   257→
   258→    def _check_assignments(self):
   259→        assigned = set()
   260→        all_names = self.circuit.get_all_names()
   261→
   262→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   263→            if asn.lhs not in all_names:
   264→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   265→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
   266→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   267→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
   268→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   269→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
   270→            self._check_expression(asn.rhs, asn.line)
   271→
   272→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
   273→            if sig.name not in assigned:
   274→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
   275→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
   276→
   277→    def _check_expression(self, expr, line_num: int):
   278→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   279→            if expr.name not in self.circuit.get_all_names():
   280→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.filename, line_num)
   281→        elif isinstance(expr, Call):
   282→            self._check_operator_arity(expr, line_num)
   283→            for arg in expr.args:
   284→                self._check_expression(arg, line_num)
   285→
   286→    def _check_operator_arity(self, call: Call, line_num: int):
   287→        op = call.operator
   288→        num_args = len(call.args)
   289→
   290→        if op not in OPERATOR_ARITY:
   291→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Unknown operator: {op}", self.filename, line_num)
   292→
   293→        min_args, max_args = OPERATOR_ARITY[op]
   294→        if num_args < min_args or (max_args and num_args > max_args):
   295→            req = f"exactly {min_args}" if max_args == min_args else f"at least {min_args}"
   296→            raise CircError("ArityError", f"Operator {op} requires {req} argument{'s' if min_args > 1 else ''}, got {num_args}", self.filename, line_num)
   297→
   298→    def _check_cycles(self):
   299→        dependencies = {sig.name: set() for sig in self.circuit.inputs}
   300→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   301→            dependencies[asn.lhs] = self._get_dependencies(asn.rhs)
   302→
   303→        WHITE, GRAY, BLACK = 0, 1, 2
   304→        color = {name: WHITE for name in self.circuit.get_all_names()}
   305→
   306→        def dfs(node: str, path: list):
   307→            color[node] = GRAY
   308→            path.append(node)
   309→            for dep in dependencies.get(node, set()):
   310→                if color[dep] == GRAY:
   311→                    cycle_start = path.index(dep)
   312→                    return path[cycle_start:] + [dep]
   313→                if color[dep] == WHITE:
   314→                    result = dfs(dep, path)
   315→                    if result:
   316→                        return result
   317→            path.pop()
   318→            color[node] = BLACK
   319→            return None
   320→
   321→        for node in self.circuit.get_all_names():
   322→            if color[node] == WHITE:
   323→                cycle = dfs(node, [])
   324→                if cycle:
   325→                    raise CircError("CycleError", f"Cycle detected: {' -> '.join(cycle)}", self.filename)
   326→
   327→    def _get_dependencies(self, expr) -> set:
   328→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   329→            return {expr.name}
   330→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   331→            deps = set()
   332→            for arg in expr.args:
   333→                deps.update(self._get_dependencies(arg))
   334→            return deps
   335→        return set()
   336→
   337→
   338→class Evaluator:
   339→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
   340→        self.circuit = circuit
   341→        self.values = {}
   342→
   343→    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
   344→        self.values = dict(inputs)
   345→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   346→            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
   347→        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}
   348→
   349→    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> int:
   350→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   351→            return int(expr.value)
   352→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   353→            return self.values[expr.name]
   354→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   355→            args = [self._eval_expr(arg) for arg in expr.args]
   356→            return self._apply_operator(expr.operator, args)
   357→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   358→
   359→    def _apply_operator(self, op: str, args: list) -> int:
   360→        if op == 'NOT':
   361→            return 1 - args[0]
   362→        if op == 'BUF':
   363→            return args[0]
   364→        ops = {'AND': (operator.and_, 1), 'OR': (operator.or_, 0), 'XOR': (operator.xor, 0)}
   365→        if op in ops:
   366→            fn, init = ops[op]
   367→            result = init
   368→            for a in args:
   369→                result = fn(result, a)
   370→            return result
   371→        if op in ('NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
   372→            base_op = op[1:]
   373→            return 1 - self._apply_operator(base_op, args)
   374→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   375→
   376→
   377→def output_json(data: dict):
   378→    print(json.dumps(data, separators=(',', ':')))
   379→
   380→
   381→def load_circuit(filename: str, command: str, json_mode: bool):
   382→    """Read, parse, and validate a circuit file. Returns Circuit or exit code int."""
   383→    try:
   384→        with open(filename, 'r') as f:
   385→            content = f.read()
   386→    except (FileNotFoundError, IOError):
   387→        return output_error(CircError("FileNotFoundError", f"Cannot read file: {filename}", filename), command, json_mode)
   388→
   389→    try:
   390→        circuit = Parser(content, filename).parse()
   391→        Validator(circuit, filename).validate()
   392→    except CircError as e:
   393→        return output_error(e, command, json_mode)
   394→
   395→    return circuit
   396→
   397→
   398→def signal_list(signals):
   399→    return [{"name": s.name, "msb": s.msb, "lsb": s.lsb} for s in sorted(signals, key=lambda s: s.name)]
   400→
   401→
   402→def get_exit_code(error_type: str) -> int:
   403→    if error_type in VALIDATION_ERRORS:
   404→        return EXIT_VALIDATION_ERROR
   405→    return ERROR_EXIT_CODES.get(error_type, 1)
   406→
   407→
   408→def output_error(error, command: str, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   409→    exit_code = get_exit_code(error.error_type)
   410→    if json_mode:
   411→        output_json({"ok": False, "command": command, "exit_code": exit_code, "error": error.to_dict()})
   412→    else:
   413→        loc = ""
   414→        if hasattr(error, 'file') and error.file:
   415→            loc = f"{error.file}:"
   416→            if error.line:
   417→                loc += f"{error.line}:"
   418→                if error.col:
   419→                    loc += f"{error.col}:"
   420→            loc += " "
   421→        print(f"{loc}Error: {error.message}", file=sys.stderr)
   422→    return exit_code
   423→
   424→
   425→def check_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   426→    if not args:
   427→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   428→
   429→    filename = args[0]
   430→    result = load_circuit(filename, "check", json_mode)
   431→    if isinstance(result, int):
   432→        return result
   433→    circuit = result
   434→
   435→    inputs = signal_list(circuit.inputs)
   436→    outputs = signal_list(circuit.outputs)
   437→
   438→    if json_mode:
   439→        output_json({"ok": True, "command": "check", "format": "circ", "inputs": inputs, "outputs": outputs})
   440→    else:
   441→        print("Circuit is valid.")
   442→        print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in inputs)}")
   443→        print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in outputs)}")
   444→
   445→    return 0
   446→
   447→
   448→def eval_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   449→    if not args:
   450→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   451→
   452→    filename = args[0]
   453→
   454→    set_values = {}
   455→    default_value = None
   456→    allow_extra = False
   457→
   458→    i = 1
   459→    while i < len(args):
   460→        arg = args[i]
   461→        if arg == '--set':
   462→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
   463→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--set requires a name=value argument"), "eval", json_mode)
   464→            set_arg = args[i + 1]
   465→            if '=' not in set_arg:
   466→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --set format: {set_arg}, expected name=value"), "eval", json_mode)
   467→            name, value = set_arg.split('=', 1)
   468→            if value not in ('0', '1'):
   469→                return output_error(EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{name}': '{value}', must be 0 or 1"), "eval", json_mode)
   470→            set_values[name] = int(value)
   471→            i += 2
   472→        elif arg == '--default':
   473→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
   474→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--default requires a value (0 or 1)"), "eval", json_mode)
   475→            default_arg = args[i + 1]
   476→            if default_arg not in ('0', '1'):
   477→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --default value: {default_arg}, must be 0 or 1"), "eval", json_mode)
   478→            default_value = int(default_arg)
   479→            i += 2
   480→        elif arg == '--allow-extra':
   481→            allow_extra = True
   482→            i += 1
   483→        else:
   484→            return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Unknown option: {arg}"), "eval", json_mode)
   485→
   486→    result = load_circuit(filename, "eval", json_mode)
   487→    if isinstance(result, int):
   488→        return result
   489→    circuit = result
   490→
   491→    input_names = {sig.name for sig in circuit.inputs}
   492→
   493→    for name in set_values:
   494→        if name not in input_names and not allow_extra:
   495→            return output_error(EvalError("UnknownInputError", f"Unknown input: {name}"), "eval", json_mode)
   496→
   497→    missing_inputs = input_names - set(set_values.keys())
   498→    if missing_inputs and default_value is None:
   499→        return output_error(EvalError("MissingInputError", f"Missing input values for: {', '.join(sorted(missing_inputs))}"), "eval", json_mode)
   500→
   501→    inputs = {sig.name: set_values.get(sig.name, default_value) for sig in circuit.inputs if sig.name in set_values or default_value is not None}
   502→
   503→    try:
   504→        results = Evaluator(circuit).evaluate(inputs)
   505→    except Exception as e:
   506→        return output_error(CircError("InternalError", str(e)), "eval", json_mode)
   507→
   508→    sorted_outputs = sorted(results.items(), key=lambda x: x[0])
   509→
   510→    if json_mode:
   511→        output_list = [{"name": name, "msb": 0, "lsb": 0, "value": str(value)} for name, value in sorted_outputs]
   512→        output_json({"ok": True, "command": "eval", "mode": "2val", "radix": "bin", "outputs": output_list})
   513→    else:
   514→        for name, value in sorted_outputs:
   515→            print(f"{name}={value}")
   516→
   517→    return 0
   518→
   519→
   520→def main():
   521→    args = sys.argv[1:]
   522→
   523→    if '--help' in args:
   524→        print("""Usage: circopt.py [OPTIONS] <COMMAND> [ARGS]
   525→
   526→Options:
   527→  --help     Show this help message and exit
   528→  --version  Show version and exit
   529→  --json     Output in JSON format
   530→
   531→Commands:
   532→  check      Validate a .circ circuit file
   533→  eval       Evaluate a circuit with given inputs
   534→
   535→Run 'circopt.py <COMMAND> --help' for more information on a command.""")
   536→        return 0
   537→
   538→    if '--version' in args:
   539→        json_mode = '--json' in args
   540→        if json_mode:
   541→            output_json({"ok": True, "command": "__version__", "version": VERSION})
   542→        else:
   543→            print(VERSION)
   544→        return 0
   545→
   546→    json_mode = '--json' in args
   547→    args = [a for a in args if a != '--json']
   548→
   549→    if not args:
   550→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "No command provided. Use --help for usage information."), "__cli__", json_mode)
   551→
   552→    command, *command_args = args
   553→
   554→    if command == 'check':
   555→        return check_command(command_args, json_mode)
   556→    if command == 'eval':
   557→        return eval_command(command_args, json_mode)
   558→    return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Unknown command: {command}"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   559→
   560→
   561→if __name__ == '__main__':
   562→    sys.exit(main())
   563→

     6→import re

    17→    "DeclarationAfterAssignmentError", "DuplicateNameError", "UndefinedNameError",
    18→    "UnassignedSignalError", "InputAssignmentError", "MultipleAssignmentError",

    31→class CircError(Exception):
    32→    def __init__(self, error_type: str, message: str, file: str = None, line: int = None, col: int = None):
    33→        super().__init__(message)
    34→        self.error_type = error_type
    35→        self.message = message
    36→        self.file = file

    38→        self.col = col
